title: Quick check: Pixel and Conversions API

questions.prompt: Why does CAPI matter more than pixel alone?

choices.text: Pixel fires from the browser and is subject to iOS 14.5 ATT, Safari ITP, ad blockers, and privacy extensions — losing 20-40% of events. CAPI sends events server-to-server, bypassing browser-side suppression, and carries clean hashed user data (email, phone, name, IDs) that boost Event Match Quality. Used together with deduplication via event_id, pixel + CAPI gives the most complete signal — typically 10-25% lift in attributed conversions after enabling CAPI.

questions.explanation: Pixel misses iOS users; CAPI reaches Meta server-to-server. Use both with event_id dedupe.

questions.prompt: Reported ROAS is 4x; actual revenue from analytics shows 2x. Likely cause?

choices.text: Deduplication failure between pixel and CAPI. Both are firing Purchase events but without a shared event_id, Meta counts each separately — doubling the recorded conversions and inflating ROAS. Fix: generate a unique event_id (commonly the order ID from your e-commerce platform) and send it in both the pixel fire and the CAPI fire. Meta will dedupe on that key. Verify in Events Manager that combined Purchase count ≈ actual order count, not 2x.

questions.explanation: Missing event_id = double-count. Always send shared event_id (order ID works).
